title: The Enemy by Pearl S. Buck
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Conflict between human compassion and national loyalty, duty as a doctor versus duty as a citizen, and humanity rising above hatred.

summary:
The story is set during World War II and revolves around Dr. Sadao Hoki, a Japanese surgeon trained in America, and his wife Hana. One night, they find an American soldier washed up on the beach near their house. He is a wounded prisoner of war with a gunshot wound and is bleeding heavily. Though Sadao knows that hiding an enemy is treason and punishable by death in Japan, his duty as a doctor compels him to save the man. He brings the soldier home, and he and Hana secretly operate on him. They remove the bullet and nurse him back to health.

The servants in their house are horrified and leave, calling Sadao a traitor. Even Hana is conflicted, torn between loyalty to her country and basic human compassion. Sadao also struggles. He is patriotic and knows the soldier is the enemy, yet he cannot let a man die when he has the skill to save him.

To resolve the conflict, Sadao informs the old General he is treating. The General promises to send private assassins to kill the soldier quietly. But the General forgets because he is self-centered and cares mainly about his own health. Realizing the danger, Sadao decides to help the soldier escape. He gives him a boat, food, water, clothes, and instructions to wait for a Korean fishing boat. He tells him to signal with a flashlight if he runs out of food. After the soldier leaves, Sadao waits each night but sees no light. He understands that the man has escaped safely.
